title: Easter Sunday
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## April 20, 2025 | Preacher: Richard Lawson

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

Easter Sunday 2025 falls on April 20, marking the principal feast of the Christian year. The Revised Common Lectionary readings would be Acts 10:34-43 (or Isaiah 65:17-25), Psalm 118:1-2, 14-24, 1 Corinthians 15:1-11 (or Colossians 3:1-4), and John 20:1-18 (or Matthew 28:1-10). This is the climax of the Easter Triduum and the beginning of the fifty-day Easter season, with the cathedral likely featuring full festive liturgy including the renewal of baptismal vows.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church remains navigating its post-2024 General Convention trajectory, where significant debates over LGBTQ+ inclusion and theological boundaries continued to shape diocesan life. The Diocese of Colorado would be in spring operations mode with Easter being its largest attendance Sunday, though no major diocesan elections or crisis events are documented for April 2025. Bishop Kym Cole (who became bishop of Colorado in 2023) would typically be presiding at major cathedral liturgies, though specific confirmation of her role on this date is not available.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver weather in mid-April 2025 would typically be transitional spring, with possible snow or severe weather still possible at high elevation (April being one of Colorado's snowier months historically). The Denver metro area would be focused on spring events and outdoor season preparation. No major documented local crises or political upheaval specific to the week of April 13-20, 2025 are available, though the Cathedral sits in the Capitol Hill neighborhood, a densely urban area with ongoing housing and homelessness discussions.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

Without access to verified April 2025 news, the general national environment would likely involve the 2025 political cycle dynamics (mid-first term or post-2024 election period), ongoing economic conditions, and spring weather events. Major institutional and political divisions that characterized 2024-2025 would likely still be prominent. Easter itself creates a countercultural moment of Christian witness amid secular spring holiday observance.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Major international stories from spring 2025 are not available in verified form. Ongoing conflicts in Ukraine, Middle East tensions, and global economic developments would likely dominate international attention as they had through early 2025. Easter observance in global Anglican Communion would create moment of communion across geopolitical divides.

CULTURAL MOMENT

Easter 2025 represents the annual Christian counter-narrative to secular culture, with American spring break culture and commercial Easter observance running parallel to liturgical solemnity. Denver's growing cultural diversity and increasing secularization would create particular tension between cathedral as living faith community and cultural institution. The general American mood in spring 2025 reflected persistent polarization and institutional skepticism, making the Easter proclamation of resurrection and hope potentially countercultural and urgent.
